clade 0.1.0

  • Reference
    • Releases
    • Changelog
Agent-based simulation of evolution and foraging ecology. Digital organisms carry heritable neural-network genomes (diploid or haploid) and one of six brain types: Bayesian neural network (default), multilayer perceptron, continuous-time RNN, gene regulatory network, transformer, or symbolic rule synthesis. Agents evolve over generations via natural selection on behaviour, life history, and cognition. The simulation runs entirely in Julia (via JuliaConnectoR), eliminating the per-tick R-backend boundary overhead and enabling runs of 100,000 agents. Provides MAP-Elites, CMA-ES (pure R, no external dependencies), and finite-difference gradient parameter search.
